title: Document arrived: Q3-2025 Quarterly Filing — Meridian Logistics SA
detail: 3 pages · dated 2025-11-10 · Sentinel waking up

title: Triage
detail: Nemotron Nano classifies the arrival before waking the full agent

title: Triage: quarterly filing → investigate
detail: The filing reports financial position and covenant‑relevant items such as liquidity, EBITDA impact, and facility drawdown.

payload.reason: The filing reports financial position and covenant‑relevant items such as liquidity, EBITDA impact, and facility drawdown.

title: Planning
detail: Sentinel drafts its investigation plan

title: Investigation plan
detail: Assess Q3-2025 covenant compliance, validate the revolver drawing for electrification capex, and evaluate liquidity and earnings quality risks following exceptional charges and contract loss.

payload.goal: Assess Q3-2025 covenant compliance, validate the revolver drawing for electrification capex, and evaluate liquidity and earnings quality risks following exceptional charges and contract loss.
steps: Request and review the Q3-2025 compliance certificate to calculate net leverage, interest cover, and any capex or net debt covenants against facility thresholds.
Verify the September 2025 Revolving Facility drawing (EQUIP-PURCH-0912) against permitted uses, sub-limits, and borrowing base conditions in the Facility Agreement.
Analyse the Aurora Freight litigation settlement (LEGAL-SETTLE-0847) for consistency with prior provisions, confirm finality, and identify any residual contingent liabilities.
Assess quarter-end liquidity of EUR 3,800 thousand against minimum liquidity covenants and short-term debt service requirements.
Evaluate the non-renewal of the regional distribution contract and management's Q4-2025 margin recovery assumptions for sustainability.
Reconcile total borrowings of EUR 85,200 thousand with the EUR 40,000,000 Volta facility to identify other debt and confirm security/priority position.

title: Investigating
detail: Multi-step evidence gathering: documents, ratios, ledger

title: Sentinel reasons
detail: I need to verify the covenant thresholds in the executed facility documentation before calculating ratios. The Q3-2025 filing shows net debt of EUR 81.4m and LTM EBITDA of EUR 22.0m, suggesting a leverage ratio around 3.7x, and LTM interest of EUR 4.6m plus principal of EUR 4.0m for debt service coverage. My first step is to search the facility agreement for the exact financial covenant clauses (leverage and DSCR/interest cover) so I can read the precise thresholds.

I must read the full clause text on that page before computing ratios, per the hard rule to verify thresholds against executed documentation and not rely on snippets.

 "text": "SECTION 7.1 — FINANCIAL COVENANTS\n\n7.1(a) Leverage. The Borrower shall ensure that the Leverage Ratio in respect of any Measurement Period ending on or after 30 June 2023 does not exceed 3.50:1.00.\n\n7.1(b) Debt Service Coverage. The Borr …[truncated 412 chars]

payload.heading: SECTION 7.1 — FINANCIAL COVENANTS
payload.text:
SECTION 7.1 — FINANCIAL COVENANTS

7.1(a) Leverage. The Borrower shall ensure that the Leverage Ratio in respect of any Measurement Period ending on or after 30 June 2023 does not exceed 3.50:1.00.

7.1(b) Debt Service Coverage. The Borrower shall ensure that the ratio of Consolidated EBITDA to Debt Service in respect of any Measurement Period is not less than 1.25:1.00.

The financial covenants in this Section 7.1 shall be tested quarterly by reference to each Measurement Period ending on the last day of each Financial Quarter, on the basis of the quarterly financial statements and the related Compliance Certificate.

title: Sentinel reasons
detail: I have verified the exact covenant thresholds on page 7 of the credit agreement: leverage must not exceed 3.50:1.00 and DSCR must not be less than 1.25:1.00. The filing provides net debt of EUR 81.4m and LTM EBITDA of EUR 22.0m. My next step is to call calc_ratio for the leverage covenant for Q3-2025, followed by DSCR, then query the ledger for material movements before drafting the memo.

detail: {"metric":"leverage","quarter":"Q3-2025"}

title: calc_ratio → result
Sentinel reasons
detail: Leverage ratio is 3.7x, breaching the 3.50:1.00 threshold from page 7 of the credit agreement. I must now calculate the DSCR for Q3-2025 to check the second financial covenant before querying the ledger and drafting the memo.

title: calc_ratio
detail: {"metric":"dscr","quarter":"Q3-2025"}

title: calc_ratio → result

payload.formula: Consolidated EBITDA (LTM) / (LTM interest paid + LTM scheduled principal)

title: Sentinel reasons
detail: Leverage is 3.7x, breaching the 3.50:1.00 threshold; DSCR is 2.56x, compliant with the 1.25:1.00 floor. To attribute causes of deterioration and gather evidence for the escalation memo, I need to surface material ledger movements in Q3-2025. A single query with minAbsAmountEur=200000 across the quarter will capture the litigation settlement, electrification purchase, and other large items.

title: Sentinel reasons
detail: I have verified the exact covenant thresholds on page 7 of the 2023 Senior Facilities Agreement (leverage ≤3.50:1.00; DSCR ≥1.25:1.00). calc_ratio confirms a leverage breach at 3.70x and DSCR compliance at 2.56x. The ledger query surfaces the material cash movements that explain the deterioration: the EUR 1.2m Aurora Freight litigation settlement (LEGAL-SETTLE-0847), the EUR 2.8m electrification capex (EQUIP-PURCH-0912) funded by a EUR 2.8m revolver draw (REVOLVER-DRAW-0901), and the EUR 1.1375m quarterly interest payment (INT-PAY-0930). The filing notes an additional ~EUR 0.8m EBITDA erosion from fuel costs and a lost contract, with liquidity falling to EUR 3.8m. Total borrowings of EUR 85.2m against the EUR 40m Volta facility signals significant other debt. I have exhausted my initial tool budget and have sufficient evidence to draft an escalation-grade memo.

title: Drafting memo
detail: Writing memo from the investigation log

title: Draft memo: BREACH
detail: 5 findings · 8/8 citations verified verbatim

payload.title: Covenant Monitoring Memo — Meridian Logistics SA Q3-2025
payload.summary: Q3-2025 leverage is 3.70x, breaching the 3.50x covenant ceiling. DSCR remains compliant at 2.56x. The deterioration reflects the EUR 1.2m Aurora Freight litigation settlement, EUR 2.8m electrification capex funded via revolver draw, and an estimated EUR 0.8m EBITDA erosion from fuel costs and a lost contract. Liquidity declined to EUR 3.8m. Total borrowings of EUR 85.2m indicate material non-Volta debt.

findings.text: Leverage ratio is 3.70x, breaching the 3.50:1.00 ceiling.

citations.quote: does not exceed 3.50:1.00

elevated fuel costs and the non-renewal of a regional distribution contract, together reducing EBITDA by approximately EUR 800 thousand relative to the prior quarter

findings.text: EUR 2.8m fleet electrification capex was funded through a September revolver draw, increasing gross debt.

citations.quote: purchase of twelve electric trucks and associated charging infrastructure from Nordvolt Fleet Systems for EUR 2,800 thousand (reference EQUIP-PURCH-0912), funded through a drawing under the Revolving Facility in September 2025

findings.text: Liquidity declined to EUR 3.8m and total borrowings rose to EUR 85.2m.

citations.quote:
Cash and cash equivalents: EUR 3,800 thousand
Total borrowings (incl. revolver drawings): EUR 85,200 thousand

flaggedTransactions.cause: Revolving facility drawdown to fund electrification capex
Purchase of 12 electric trucks and charging infrastructure from Nordvolt Fleet Systems
Final settlement of Aurora Freight SARL litigation

payload.recommendation: Escalate to relationship manager immediately. Request a draft compliance certificate and equity cure notice if applicable. Verify the identity, security and amortisation profile of non-Volta debt in the EUR 85.2m total borrowings. Require a 13-week cash flow forecast and confirm the revolver draw for capex was permitted.

title: The Skeptic reviews (round 1)
detail: Adversarial internal challenge before anything reaches a human

title: Objection OBJ-1: Threshold basis — Amendment Agreement not checked
detail: The memo relies on the 3.50x leverage threshold from the original 2023 Senior Facilities Agreement, but the investigation log does not show any search for or review of an Amendment Agreement that might modify this threshold. Amendments prevail over the original agreement per the checklist. Without verifying whether an amendment exists and whether it changes the tested period's threshold, the breach conclusion is unsupported.

payload.target: Threshold basis — Amendment Agreement not checked
payload.objection: The memo relies on the 3.50x leverage threshold from the original 2023 Senior Facilities Agreement, but the investigation log does not show any search for or review of an Amendment Agreement that might modify this threshold. Amendments prevail over the original agreement per the checklist. Without verifying whether an amendment exists and whether it changes the tested period's threshold, the breach conclusion is unsupported.
payload.requiredAction: Search for any Amendment Agreement to the 2023 Senior Facilities Agreement, read its financial covenant clauses, and confirm whether the 3.50x threshold applies to Q3-2025 or has been amended.

title: Objection OBJ-2: Completeness — material unexplained movements in total borrowings
detail: The memo notes total borrowings of EUR 85.2m against a EUR 40m Volta facility, indicating EUR 45.2m of non-Volta debt, but the investigation log does not identify or document the causes of this material amount. The ledger query only shows transactions up to EUR 2.8m, leaving the bulk of borrowings unexplained. This is a material unexplained movement that must be matched to documented causes or explicitly carried as an outstanding item.

payload.target: Completeness — material unexplained movements in total borrowings
payload.objection: The memo notes total borrowings of EUR 85.2m against a EUR 40m Volta facility, indicating EUR 45.2m of non-Volta debt, but the investigation log does not identify or document the causes of this material amount. The ledger query only shows transactions up to EUR 2.8m, leaving the bulk of borrowings unexplained. This is a material unexplained movement that must be matched to documented causes or explicitly carried as an outstanding item.
payload.requiredAction: Query the ledger or other documents to identify the composition and causes of the EUR 45.2m non-Volta borrowings, and either match them to documented debt instruments or note them as outstanding items requiring further investigation.

title: Resolving objections
detail: Sentinel goes back to the evidence

title: Sentinel reasons
detail: OBJ-1 is threshold-critical: if an amendment raised the leverage ceiling, the 3.70x result may not be a breach. I must search for any Amendment Agreement to the 2023 Senior Facilities Agreement before the breach conclusion can stand. OBJ-2 (non-Volta debt composition) is material but secondary to whether a breach exists at all. The next best action is a search for amendments.

title: Sentinel reasons
detail: The search reveals Amendment Agreement No. 2 (dated 20 June 2025) with a 'Covenant Holiday' that expressly overrides Section 7.1(a) for the Measurement Periods ending 30 September 2025 and 31 December 2025. Since Q3-2025 ends on 30 September 2025, the original 3.50x threshold may not apply. I must read page 2 of amendment-2 to capture the exact amended leverage threshold before confirming or retracting the breach conclusion.

 "text": "AMENDMENT AGREEMENT NO. 2 — OPERATIVE PROVISIONS\n\nClause 2.1 (Covenant Holiday). Notwithstanding Section 7.1(a) of the Agreement, for the Measurement Periods ending 30 September 2025 and 31 December 2025 only, the Borrower s …[truncated 620 chars]

payload.heading: AMENDMENT AGREEMENT NO. 2 — OPERATIVE PROVISIONS
payload.text:
AMENDMENT AGREEMENT NO. 2 — OPERATIVE PROVISIONS

Clause 2.1 (Covenant Holiday). Notwithstanding Section 7.1(a) of the Agreement, for the Measurement Periods ending 30 September 2025 and 31 December 2025 only, the Borrower shall ensure that the Leverage Ratio does not exceed 4.00:1.00.

Clause 2.2 (Reversion). For each Measurement Period ending on or after 31 March 2026, the ratio in Section 7.1(a) shall revert to 3.50:1.00 without further action of the parties.

Clause 2.3 (Minimum Liquidity Condition). During the period referred to in Clause 2.1, the Borrower shall maintain Cash and Cash Equivalents of not less than EUR 3,000,000, tested on the last day of each Financial Quarter.

Clause 2.4 This Amendment Agreement is a Finance Document and prevails over the Agreement to the extent of any inconsistency.

title: Sentinel reasons
detail: OBJ-1 is resolved: Amendment Agreement No. 2 (dated 20 June 2025) raises the leverage threshold to 4.00:1.00 for Q3-2025 (Measurement Period ending 30 September 2025). The calculated leverage of 3.70x is below this amended threshold, so there is no leverage breach. For OBJ-2, I need to identify the composition of the EUR 45.2m non-Volta borrowings (EUR 85.2m total less EUR 40m Volta facility). The Q3-2025 filing log does not break this down, so I must search other indexed documents for a debt schedule or other debt instruments.

title: Sentinel reasons
detail: OBJ-1 resolved: Amendment No. 2 (page 2) raises Q3-2025 leverage threshold to 4.00x and imposes EUR 3.0m minimum liquidity; 3.70x leverage and EUR 3.8m liquidity are compliant. OBJ-2: Searched indexed documents and ledger; no breakdown of EUR 45.2m non-Volta borrowings found, so carried as outstanding item. All objections addressed.

title: Final memo: EARLY_WARNING
detail: Covenant Monitoring Memo — Meridian Logistics SA Q3-2025

memo.title: Covenant Monitoring Memo — Meridian Logistics SA Q3-2025
memo.summary: Q3-2025 leverage is 3.70x, compliant under the Amendment No. 2 covenant holiday threshold of 4.00x. DSCR is 2.56x, above 1.25x. Liquidity of EUR 3.8m meets the EUR 3.0m minimum condition. The quarter absorbed a EUR 1.2m litigation settlement, EUR 2.8m electrification capex funded by revolver draw, and an estimated EUR 0.8m EBITDA hit from fuel costs and a lost contract. Total borrowings of EUR 85.2m include material non-Volta debt; composition remains unidentified.

findings.text: Leverage ratio of 3.70x is compliant for Q3-2025 because Amendment No. 2 raised the threshold to 4.00:1.00 for the Measurement Period ending 30 September 2025.

citations.quote: for the Measurement Periods ending 30 September 2025 and 31 December 2025 only, the Borrower shall ensure that the Leverage Ratio does not exceed 4.00:1.00

Total net debt: EUR 81,400 thousand
LTM EBITDA (last twelve months): EUR 22,000 thousand

findings.text: DSCR of 2.56x exceeds the 1.25:1.00 minimum under the original agreement.

citations.quote: not less than 1.25:1.00

LTM interest paid: EUR 4,600 thousand
LTM scheduled principal repayments: EUR 4,000 thousand

findings.text: Quarter-end liquidity of EUR 3.8m satisfies the Amendment No. 2 minimum liquidity condition of EUR 3.0m.

citations.quote: the Borrower shall maintain Cash and Cash Equivalents of not less than EUR 3,000,000, tested on the last day of each Financial Quarter
Cash and cash equivalents: EUR 3,800 thousand

findings.text: EBITDA was reduced by a EUR 1.2m litigation settlement and an estimated EUR 0.8m from fuel costs and a lost contract.

citations.quote: EUR 1,200 thousand charge in operating expenses in connection with the final settlement of the Aurora Freight SARL litigation
elevated fuel costs and the non-renewal of a regional distribution contract, together reducing EBITDA by approximately EUR 800 thousand relative to the prior quarter

findings.text: Total borrowings of EUR 85.2m could not be reconciled to specific debt instruments in the available documents; the composition remains an outstanding item.

citations.quote: Total borrowings (incl. revolver drawings): EUR 85,200 thousand

flaggedTransactions.cause: Revolving facility drawdown to fund electrification capex
Purchase of 12 electric trucks and charging infrastructure from Nordvolt Fleet Systems
Final settlement of Aurora Freight SARL litigation

memo.recommendation: Request a 13-week cash flow forecast and confirm the revolver draw for capex was permitted under the Facility Agreement. Investigate the composition and security position of total borrowings. Monitor Q4-2025 margin recovery and covenant reversion to 3.50x.

components.label: Covenant clause read in executed agreement
Amendments checked for the tested period
Flagged movements matched to documented causes
